Box 1200, Ridgewood, Nevada 89502, United States of America - hereinafter referred to as “ Vectron Systems Inc. ” - RECITALS WHEREAS, Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding holds a majority interest in Vectron Systems Fab 36 Limited Liability Company & Co. KG having its registered office in Dresden (“ Vectron Systems Fab 36 ”). Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding is in turn a wholly-owned Subsidiary of Vectron Systems Inc.; WHEREAS, Vectron Systems Inc., Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding and Vectron Systems Fab 36 are currently involved in the planning and implementation stages of a project pursuant to which Vectron Systems Fab 36 will use and develop to the production stage semiconductor manufacturing technology and produce 300 mm Wafers, in particular, for microprocessors, in a newly to be constructed wafer plant in Dresden; WHEREAS, concurrently with conclusion of this Agreement (this “ Agreement ”), Vectron Systems Fab 36 and Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding are entering into an Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement (as amended from time to time the “ Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement ”) pursuant to which Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding is obtaining the exclusive right to purchase Wafers from Vectron Systems Fab 36 and Vectron Systems Fab 36 is agreeing, on such exclusive basis, to manufacture and sell Wafers to Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding, in each case on the terms and conditions of the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement; WHEREAS, on the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Vectron Systems Inc. wishes the exclusive right to purchase all such Wafers as are purchased by Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding from Vectron Systems Fab 36 on the terms and conditions of the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement, and Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding is willing, on such exclusive basis, to sell such Wafers to Vectron Systems Inc.; and WHEREAS, on 31 October 2003, Vectron Systems Inc., Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding GmbH, Vectron Systems Fab 36 and Vectron Systems Saxony Limited Liability Company & Co. KG, Dresden (“Vectron Systems Saxony”) entered into a Management Service Agreement (“ Fab 36 Management Service Agreement ”) and Vectron Systems Inc., Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding GmbH, and Vectron Systems Fab 36 entered into a license agreement. NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of above, the parties hereto agree as follows: ARTICLE I Purchase of Products Section 1.01. Sale of Products by Vectron Systems Fab 36 to Vectron Systems Inc. . (a) On the terms and subject to the conditions of this Agreement, Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding hereby agrees to sell to Vectron Systems Inc. 100% of such Products as are manufactured by Vectron Systems Fab 36 and sold to Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding on the terms and subject to the conditions of the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement. Products within the meaning of this Agreement are all Wafers manufactured by Vectron Systems Fab 36. Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding further agrees that, during the term of this Agreement, unless Vectron Systems Inc. and, prior to the Loan Agreement Termination Date, the Facility Agent, otherwise consent thereto, it shall not, directly or indirectly, (i) sell any Products or any other goods or services to any Person other than Vectron Systems Inc. or one or more of Vectron Systems Inc.’s Subsidiaries under any circumstances, or (ii) order products from Vectron Systems Fab 36 which have not been ordered by Vectron Systems Inc. from Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding hereunder, (iii) amend, supplement or otherwise modify the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement, (iv) engage in any activities other than (a) the investment in equity of Vectron Systems Fab 36, (b) the making of loans and the provision of other financial support to Vectron Systems Fab 36, (c) the purchase and sale of Products as contemplated hereby and (d) activities reasonably incidental to any of the foregoing. (b) On the terms and subject to the conditions of this Agreement, Vectron Systems Inc. hereby agrees to purchase from Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding all Products manufactured by Vectron Systems Fab 36 and sold to Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding in accordance with the terms of the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement and to pay for such Products the purchase price determined pursuant to Article II. (c) Vectron Systems Inc. shall from time to time provide, for and on behalf of Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding, or shall arrange for one or more of its Subsidiaries to provide, in particular, but not only under the Management Service Agreement, to Vectron Systems Fab 36 technical assistance as required in Article III of the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement (including the disclosure stated therein and the permission to make copies and assign employees to Fab 30). Irrespective of any fault, Vectron Systems Inc. warrants by means of an independent promise of warranty pursuant to Section 311 para. 1 German Civil Code that Wafers which meet the requirements can be manufactured in a timely manner with the provided technical assistance and the rights on intellectual property granted according to Section 3 b) of the License Agreement and the technical documentation. The exclusive legal consequence of the warranty irrespective of fault is that Vectron Systems Inc. cannot invoke bad performance/non-performance by Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding; such warranty has no other legal consequences. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Know-how to be provided pursuant to this Section 1.01(c) and Article III of the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement shall include such of the “Specific Results” (as such term is defined in the JDA), 3 as are necessary or, in the reasonable opinion of Vectron Systems Inc., desirable, to enable Vectron Systems Fab 36 to manufacture Products on a timely basis, in accordance with the Specifications for the relevant Product. Any disclosure of Know-how and Specifications pursuant to this Section 1.01(c) or Article III of the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement (including, without limitation, Know-how consisting of Specific Results and Know-how disclosed by way of the furnishing of technical assistance) to Vectron Systems Fab 36 by or on behalf of Vectron Systems Inc. or Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding shall be subject to the License Agreement. Section 1.02. Compliance with Laws and Required Permits. Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding shall use commercially reasonable efforts to (i) comply with all applicable Laws relating to the sale of the Products, and (ii) obtain all requisite consents, authorizations, permits and approvals for the sale to Vectron Systems Inc. of the Products from each governmental authority having jurisdiction over the sale of the Products by Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding to Vectron Systems Inc.; provided, however, that Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding shall not be responsible for obtaining any consents, authorizations, permits or approvals in connection with the import of Products into any territory outside Germany unless expressly otherwise agreed by Vectron Systems Fab 36; (iii) Irrespective of any fault, Vectron Systems Inc. warrants by means of an independent promise of warranty pursuant to Section 311 para. 1 German Civil Code that the distribution of the Products is not conflicting with German law and U.S. law. The exclusive legal consequence of the warranty irrespective of fault is that Vectron Systems Inc. cannot invoke bad performance/non-performance by Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding; such warranty has no other legal consequences. Section 1.03. Quality of Products; Inspection and Rejection; Replacement Products. Without prejudice to Vectron Systems Inc.’s obligation to make payment for Products shipped pursuant to Section 2: (a) Vectron Systems Holding shall use all reasonable efforts to ensure that each Product shipped meets the Specifications for that Product. In order to assist Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding in such efforts and for the purpose of assuring to Vectron Systems Inc. the quality of the Products required under this Agreement, Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding shall permit Vectron Systems Inc. and its Subsidiaries, at any time during normal working hours and on reasonable notice, to inspect any premises of Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding, and shall use all reasonable efforts to permit such representatives, at such time and on such notice, to inspect any premises of any third party where any of the Products, or any labeling or packaging for them, are manufactured by or for Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding. Furthermore, Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding shall cause Vectron Systems Fab 36 to make the efforts described in this Section 1.03.(a) and/or to issue permissions to Vectron Systems Inc. for such inspections at Vectron Systems Fab 36 or third-party contractors of Vectron Systems Fab 36. (b) Vectron Systems Inc. shall promptly notify Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding in writing should the quality of the Products shipped vary materially from the typical quality of the four previous 4 shipments or, in the case of the first four shipments, from the quality of the previous shipments. In such event, Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding shall use its reasonable commercial efforts to restore the quality of the Products shipped hereunder to again meet such typical quality as soon as possible and will cause Vectron Systems Fab 36 to look after that. ARTICLE II Purchase Price and Delivery of Products Section 2.01. Purchase and Payment Obligation. (a) From and after the Effective Date Vectron Systems Inc. shall purchase all Products which it orders from Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding and are shipped by Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding in accordance with the requirements of this Agreement. The purchase price for the Products shall be equal the Selling Price Per Wafer as defined in Section 2.01 in conjunction with Section 2.02 of the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement. For all Products shipped during any Month, Vectron Systems Inc. shall make payments to Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding based on the Selling Price Per Wafer. In addition, Vectron Systems Inc. shall purchase from Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding any and all damage claims acquired by Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding pursuant to Section 2.06 a) of the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement from Vectron Systems Fab 36. (b) In aggregate, Vectron Systems Inc. agrees to make payments to Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding which in terms of their amount and due date shall correspond to all payments due and to be made by Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding to Vectron Systems Fab 36 under the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement. This applies, in particular, without limitation to • Advance Payments pursuant to Section 2.03 of the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement, • payment of the Selling Price Per Wafer by Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding pursuant to Section 2.02 of the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement, • annual compensation for certain administrative costs, pursuant to Section 2.04 of the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement, • costs for transportation and insurance pursuant to Section 2.05 of the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement, and 5 • any damages payable by Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding on the basis of Section 5.01 b) at the end of the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement. If and to the extent that payments are made by Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding to Vectron Systems Fab 36 against assignment of claims, such claims shall be assigned by Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding to Vectron Systems Inc. at the time when Vectron Systems Inc. effects payment, and Vectron Systems Inc. hereby accepts such assignment. Section 2.02. Shipping Terms. Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding shall deliver the Products purchased hereunder from Dresden, Germany. Pursuant to Section 2.05 of the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement, Vectron Systems Fab 36 will arrange all freight, trucking and insurance. The costs for transportation and insurance will – as far as applicable - be separately regulated by the parties on a case-by-case basis (in accordance with the regulation as agreed from time to time between Vectron Systems Fab 36 and Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding). Section 2.03. Payments; Set Off. (a) Vectron Systems Inc. shall pay to Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding the Selling Price Per Wafer for all Products shipped by Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding prior to the purchase price due date for such Products under the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement (if Vectron Systems Fab 36 or Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding notifies this reasonably in advance) within 30 days after receipt of invoices denominated in Euro from Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding in accordance with this Section 2.03. Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding shall be entitled to issue invoices to Vectron Systems Inc. on a monthly basis. (b) Payment for all other amounts due hereunder from either party (including any payments under Section 2.01 b)) shall be unconditionally due on or before the date specified in the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement for such amount. Vectron Systems Holding agrees that each and every confirmation, invoice or declaration from Vectron Systems Fab 36 to Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding which under the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement must be delivered by Vectron Systems Fab 36 to Vectron Systems Holding prior to the relevant due date will be delivered to Vectron Systems Inc. without delay and in any event prior to the relevant due date. (c) All amounts under this Agreement not paid when due from Vectron Systems Inc. shall bear interest at the rate of 3% above the three-month EURIBOR per annum on a pro rata temporis basis. Such interest shall be due and payable at the end of each calendar month. (d) Any amounts that are due from Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding to Vectron Systems Inc. hereunder shall be payable only in the form of credit against amounts owed or to be owed by Vectron Systems Inc. to Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding under this Agreement, except to the extent provided in Section 3.02 in conjunction with the Fab 36 Management Service Agreement. Any amount that is due from Vectron Systems Inc. to Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding hereunder may be set-off against any payment then due under Sections 3, 5.02, or the Fab 36 Management Service Agreement, from Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding to Vectron Systems Inc. 6 (e) Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding has the right to assign to Vectron Systems Fab 36 its payment claims against Vectron Systems Inc. under this Agreement to secure the payment claims of Vectron Systems Fab 36 against Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding under the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Costs Plus Reimbursement Agreement (including damage claims in case that, in the event of bankruptcy of Vectron Systems Inc., the trustee in bankruptcy of Vectron Systems Inc. or any other person with similar authority does not continue the Fab 36 Holding Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement, regardless of whether such damage claims result under German or U.S. state or federal law). Section 2.04. Method of Payment. Payments under this Agreement from Vectron Systems Inc. to Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding, if made other than by set-off permitted by Section 2.03, shall be deposited into an account of Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding at Dresdner Bank AG, Dresden Branch or such other account of Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding specified by Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding with the prior written consent of the Facility Agent (a copy of which consent shall be delivered by Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding to Vectron Systems Inc.) with not less than 15 days written notice to Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding, and all such payments shall be made in Euro, or in US-Dollar, or a combination of Euro and US-Dollar, in each case at the option of Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding. The relevant US-Dollar amount results from the conversion of Euro to US-Dollar at the Facility Agent’s spot rate of exchange as in effect on the date of the relevant invoice. Section 2.05. Product Warranty. Subject to Section 2.06, Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding represents and warrants that all Products sold to Vectron Systems Inc. pursuant to this Agreement shall conform in all material respects to the Specifications for such Products and, if expressly agreed by Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding in writing in advance, to any specific changes thereto. Section 2.06. Disclaimer. THE WARRANTY CONTAINED IN SECTION 2.05 IS THE SOLE AND EXCLUSIVE WARRANTY AS TO THE PRODUCTS AND IS EXPRESSLY IN LIEU OF ANY EXPRESS OR IMPLIED WARRANTIES, IN FACT OR IN LAW, INCLUDING, WITHOUT LIMITATION, WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE OR ANY SIMILAR WARRANTIES IMPLIED BY APPLICABLE LAW. REPAIR OR REPLACEMENT OF DEFECTIVE PRODUCTS SHALL BE Vectron Systems INC.’s EXCLUSIVE REMEDY WITH RESPECT TO DEFECTIVE PRODUCTS. Vectron Systems FAB 36 HOLDING ASSUMES NO LIABILITY IN TORT OR STRICT LIABILITY, NOR SHALL Vectron Systems FAB 36 HOLDING BE LIABLE TO Vectron Systems INC. OR ANY AFFILIATE THEREOF FOR LOSS OR USE OF PRODUCTS OR ANY OTHER INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES INCURRED BY Vectron Systems INC. OR ANY SUBSIDIARY OR AFFILIATE THEREOF. IN NO EVENT SHALL THE LIABILITY OF Vectron Systems FAB 36 HOLDING ARISING IN CONNECTION WITH ANY PRODUCTS SOLD HERUNDER EXCEED THE PURCHASE PRICE FOR SUCH PRODUCTS. 7 Section 2.07. Annual Compensation for Certain Administrative Costs. Vectron Systems Inc. shall compensate Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding for any costs not covered by Sections 2.01 and 2.02, including without limitation bookkeeping, legal and accounting costs, incurred by Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding from time to time in connection with this Agreement and the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement plus a markup of 7.5% on such costs. Such payment shall be made not later than 30 days after the end of each Fiscal Year. Section 2.08 Limitation of Claims. Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding in Vectron Systems Inc. agree that Vectron Systems Inc. shall only be entitled to raise claims against Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding under this Agreement if and to the extent Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding on its part can raise claims against Vectron Systems Fab 36 under the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement. For the discharge of its obligations, Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding shall be entitled to assign its claims against Vectron Systems Fab 36 to Vectron Systems Inc. ARTICLE III Technical Assistance Section 3.01. Provision of Know-how and Advice, Training and Technical Assistance, Access to Comparable Plants. Vectron Systems Inc. will provide technical assistance pursuant to Section 1.01c) hereof. Insofar as a declaration from Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding is a precondition for such obligations pursuant to Section 3 of the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement, Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding shall immediately after making such declaration inform Vectron Systems Inc. thereof. Section 3.02. Charge for Technical Assistance. (a) The remuneration for services provided under this Section 3 or under Section 1.01.c) shall be paid by Vectron Systems Fab 36 directly to Vectron Systems Inc. in accordance with the Fab 36 Management Service Agreement. (b) The foregoing notwithstanding, the parties agree that, without prejudice to the last sentence of Section 1.01(c) or to the License Agreement, any Intellectual Property provided in any form to Vectron Systems Fab 36 (or Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding for the benefit of Vectron Systems Fab 36) for its use hereunder is provided at no charge. ARTICLE IV Accounting Reports: Other Reports: Right of Inspection Section 4.01. Annual Accounting Reports from Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding, Inspection Rights. Vectron Systems Inc. and its duly authorized representatives (which may include duly authorized representatives of a Subsidiary of Vectron Systems Inc.) and, if the Loan Agreement 8 Termination Date has not yet taken place, the Facility Agent shall at all reasonable times have access to the books and accounts kept by Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding and annually upon the closing of the Fiscal Year (starting with the Fiscal Year 2004) all such books and accounts shall be audited by a firm of independent and internationally known public accountants selected by Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding with the approval of Vectron Systems Inc. and, if the Loan Agreement Termination Date has not yet taken place, the Facility Agent (the consent of the Facility Agent not to be unreasonably withheld). A copy of each such report of audit shall be sent to Vectron Systems Inc. and, prior to the Loan Agreement Termination Date, the Facility Agent. Vectron Systems Inc. and its duly authorized representatives (which may include duly authorized representatives of a Subsidiary of Vectron Systems Inc.) shall also have the right to examine and inspect at any reasonable time all properties and operations of Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding to which this Agreement relates. Section 4.02. Rights of Inspection at Vectron Systems Fab 36. Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding shall make sure that (i) Vectron Systems Inc. and its representatives (which may include a Subsidiary of Vectron Systems Inc.) and, prior to the Loan Agreement Termination Date, the Facility Agent shall at all reasonable times have access to the books and accounts kept by Vectron Systems Fab 36, (ii) all books and accounts kept by Vectron Systems Fab 36 will be audited once per year on completion of the Fiscal Year by an independent and internationally recognized auditing firm to be appointed by Vectron Systems Fab 36 with the approval of Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding and, prior to the Loan Agreement Termination Date, of the Facility Agent (with the Facility Agent’s approval not to be withheld unreasonably), (iii) a copy of the auditing report be delivered to Vectron Systems Inc. without delay, and (iv) Vectron Systems Inc. and its representatives (which may include duly authorized representatives of a Subsidiary of Vectron Systems Inc.) are authorized to inspect at all reasonable times all Plant facilities of Vectron Systems Fab 36 to which the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement relates. The rights of Leipziger Messe GmbH and of M+W Zander Fünfte Verwaltungsgesellschaft mbH (“M+W”) as direct or indirect partners of Vectron Systems Fab 36 pursuant to Article 9.3 of the Limited Partnership Agreement of Vectron Systems Fab 36 remain unaffected. ARTICLE V Effectiveness; Termination Section 5.01. Effectiveness: Termination. (a) This Agreement shall become effective on the date hereof and (unless otherwise extended) shall terminate on the date which is the earlier of (i) the effective date of the ordinary notice of termination given by any of the Parties with three months’ notice, such effective date being the end of any calendar month, however, not before the Loan Agreement Termination Date and (ii) the termination pursuant to subsection (b) sentence 1 below. No notice of ordinary termination may be given by Vectron Systems Inc. as long as Leipziger Messe GmbH and/or M+W are direct or indirect partners of Vectron Systems Fab 36, unless Leipziger Messe GmbH and/or M+W consent to such termination. 9 (b) This Agreement terminates upon termination of the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement. The right to assert statutory damage claims (Section 314 (4) BGB [German Civil Code] in conjunction with Sections 280 et seq. BGB) on the ground of termination of this Agreement for the reasons set out in Section 5.01 (b) of the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement and termination of this Agreement resulting therefrom remains unaffected. Section 5.02. Rights Upon Termination. (a) Promptly, but in no event later than 20 days following the Termination Date, (i) the actual Selling Price for the Month ending on the Termination Date shall be calculated in accordance with Section 2.02 of the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement; and (ii) if no Products were shipped in the Month ending on the Termination Date, the final Advance Payment Adjustment Amount for that Month shall be calculated in accordance with Section 2.03 of the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement. (b) Upon termination of this Agreement, Vectron Systems Inc. shall purchase, as of the Termination Date, (i) all inventory and work-in-process of Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding and (ii) all inventory and work-in-process which Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding is obliged to purchase under Section 5.02 (b) of the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement. The purchase price shall be, in the case of lit. (i) above, equal to the value thereof as shown in the books of Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding in accordance with German GAAP and, in the case of lit. (ii) above, equal to the purchase price to be paid by Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding under Section 5.02 b) of the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement. (c) Upon termination of this Agreement, all amounts shall be offset between the parties and the balance shall be settled by payment. Section 5.03. Survival. The provisions of Sections 2.05 and 2.6, 5.02, 6.02, 6.03 and 6.06 shall survive any termination of this Agreement. 10 ARTICLE VI Miscellaneous Section 6.01. Force Majeure. (a) A party to this Agreement shall not be liable for the consequences of any failure to perform, or default in performing, any of its obligations, other than its payment obligations, under this Agreement if that party can show that such failure is caused by Force Majeure. In the event that no Wafers can be manufactured due to Force Majeure, the payment obligations hereunder shall continue to be owed. (b) In case of subsection (a), the said failure shall not be considered non-compliance with any term or condition of this Agreement, and all the obligations (other than payment obligations) and times which because of such Force Majeure could not be fulfilled shall be deemed to have been suspended while the Force Majeure continues. In addition, the party for whom such obligations and/or times have been suspended shall be entitled to take reasonable steps during the pendency of the relevant Force Majeure to limit its losses resulting from such Force Majeure, and following the occurrence of such Force Majeure such obligations and/or times shall continue to be suspended for such further reasonable period as is necessary for such party to restore its capacity to perform such obligations and/or meet such times. Section 6.02. Applicable Law. This Agreement is governed by the law of the Federal Republic of Germany. Application of the UN Convention on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods of 11 April 1980 is expressly excluded. Section 6.03. Dispute Resolution . a) The parties shall endeavor to reach an amicable settlement of any dispute arising out of or in connection with this Agreement and its implementation, including any question regarding its existence, validity, termination or its performance, or in connection with arrangements regarding the performance of this Agreement. b) If any party declares that a reasonable attempt at amicable settlement of a dispute mentioned in sub-section a) has failed, such dispute shall be finally resolved by arbitration, to the exclusion of the courts of regular jurisdiction, under the Rules of Arbitration ( Schiedsgerichtsordnung ) of the German Arbitration Board (“Deutsche Institution für Schiedsgerichtswesen e.V.”) , by three arbitrators. The place of arbitration shall be Dresden, Germany. The German Code of Civil Procedure shall apply where the Rules of Arbitration are silent. c) The language to be used in the arbitration proceedings shall be German and all documents written in a language other than German shall be translated into the German language for the arbitration proceedings, unless otherwise decided by the parties to the arbitration proceedings. 11 d) The arbitral awards shall be substantiated in writing. The arbitration tribunal shall decide on the matter of costs of the arbitration and on the allocation of expenditure among the respective parties to the arbitration proceedings. Section 6.04. Definitions. It is the will of the parties hereto that each of the terms used herein and listed in the Annex 1 to the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement has the meaning specified there unless the context requires a different interpretation. Section 6.05. Assignment. None of the parties hereto shall have the right to assign its rights hereunder without the prior written consent of the other party hereto, of Leipziger Messe GmbH and of M+W as long as they are limited partners of Vectron Systems Fab 36 and, prior to the Loan Agreement Termination Date, of the Security Agent. However, Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding may assign its rights under this Agreement to the Security Agent and/or the Banks as security for obligations of Vectron Systems Fab 36 under the Loan Agreement, and the Security Agent may in turn assign such rights to any direct transferee of the Plant in the context of the proper realization of such security. Section 6.06. Language. The German version of this Agreement shall be controlling. Section 6.07 Final Provisions. a) Modifications or amendments to this Agreement shall be valid only if in writing and only with the written consent of all partners of Vectron Systems Fab 36 as far as rights or the economic position of Leipziger Messe GmbH and M+W as limited partners of Vectron Systems Fab 36 are not insignificantly adversely affected thereby, directly or indirectly, as long as Leipziger Messe GmbH or M+W are limited partners of Vectron Systems Fab 36. The partners shall be obliged to consent to amendments or modifications of this Agreement which only reflect amendments or modifications of the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement. This requirement of written form may only be waived in writing. b) If present or future terms of this Agreement are invalid or unenforceable in whole or in part or lose their validity or enforceability at a later date, this shall not affect the validity of the remaining provisions hereof. The same shall apply insofar as it 12 transpires that there is a gap in this agreement. The invalid or unenforceable provision shall be replaced, or the gap filled, by a reasonable provision which, to the extent legally permissible, comes closest to what the contracting parties intended or would have intended in accordance with the meaning and purpose of this Agreement, had they considered the point upon conclusion hereof. This also applies if the invalidity of a provision results from a degree of performance standardized hereunder (set period, date); in such cases a legally permissible degree of performance or time (set period, date), coming as close as possible to the intentions, shall replace what was agreed. In case of disputes arising among the parties hereto due to subsections a) and b) above, the arbitration tribunal (Section 6.03) shall be entitled to determine with binding force the provision which is to apply thereunder. Vectron Systems Fab 36 Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement dated April 21, 2004 Exhibit 10.70 Execution Copy (Convenience Translation) Vectron Systems FAB 36 COST PLUS REIMBURSEMENT AGREEMENT between Vectron Systems FAB 36 HOLDING GMBH Wilschdorfer Landstr. 101, 01109 Dresden - hereinafter referred to as “ Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding ” - and Vectron Systems FAB 36 LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY & CO. KG Wilschdorfer Landstr. 101, 01109 Dresden - hereinafter referred to as “ Vectron Systems Fab 36 ” - RECITALS WHEREAS, Vectron Systems Fab 36 is a majority-owned Subsidiary of Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding, which in turn is a wholly-owned Subsidiary of Vectron Systems, a corporation organized and existing under the laws of the State of Delaware, United States of America (“ Vectron Systems Inc. ”); WHEREAS, Vectron Systems Inc., Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding and Vectron Systems Fab 36 are currently involved in the planning and implementation stages of a project pursuant to which Vectron Systems Fab 36 will use and develop to the production stage semiconductor manufacturing technology and produce 300 mm Wafers, in particular, for microprocessors, in a newly to be constructed wafer plant in Dresden; WHEREAS, concurrently with conclusion of this Agreement (this “ Agreement ”), Vectron Systems Inc. and Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding are entering into the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement which is attached as Annex A and pursuant to which, among other things, Vectron Systems Inc. will agree to purchase all Wafers purchased by Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding from Vectron Systems Fab 36, and Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding will agree to supply on an exclusive basis to Vectron Systems Inc., such Wafers, in each case on the terms and conditions of the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement; WHEREAS, on the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding wishes the exclusive right to purchase Wafers from Vectron Systems Fab 36 in order to enable Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding to fulfill its obligations under the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement, and Vectron Systems Fab 36 is willing, on such exclusive basis, to manufacture and sell Wafers to Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding, all on the terms and conditions of this Agreement; and WHEREAS, Vectron Systems Inc., Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding GmbH, Vectron Systems Fab 36 and Vectron Systems Saxony Limited Liability Company & Co. KG, Dresden (“Vectron Systems Saxony”) entered into a Management Service Agreement dated October 31, 2003 (“ Fab 36 Management Service Agreement ”) and Vectron Systems Inc., Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding GmbH, and Vectron Systems Fab 36 entered into a license agreement. NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of above, the parties hereto agree as follows: I-1 ARTICLE I Manufacture of Products Section 1.01. Manufacture by Vectron Systems Fab 36 of Products for Sale to Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding. (a) On the terms and subject to the conditions of this Agreement, Vectron Systems Fab 36 hereby agrees to manufacture and sell Products to Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding. Products within the meaning of this Agreement are all Wafers manufactured by Vectron Systems Fab 36. Vectron Systems Fab 36 further agrees that, during the term of this Agreement, unless Vectron Systems Inc., the limited partners of Vectron Systems Fab 36 and, prior to the Loan Agreement Termination Date, the Facility Agent, otherwise consent thereto, it shall not, directly or indirectly, (i) sell any Products or any other goods or services to any Person other than Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding, Vectron Systems Inc. or one or more of Vectron Systems Inc.’s Subsidiaries under any circumstances, or (ii) engage in any activities other than (w) construction of the Plant, (x) manufacture and sale of Products to be Shipped pursuant to this Agreement, (y) research, development and further development of the underlying technologies to the industrial production stage, and (z) activities reasonably incidental to any of the foregoing. In the event that a further industrial manufacturing partner is admitted to Vectron Systems Fab 36, the exclusivity shall terminate with respect to manufacturing capacity of the Plant in excess of capacity dedicated to production of Wafers pursuant to this Agreement and the Parties shall agree to modify this Agreement accordingly. (b) On the terms and subject to the conditions of this Agreement, Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding hereby agrees to purchase from Vectron Systems Fab 36 all Products and to pay for all the Products Shipped to it the purchase price determined on a “cost-plus” basis pursuant to Article II. (c) Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding shall from time to time provide, or shall arrange for Vectron Systems Inc. or one or more of its Subsidiaries to provide, in particular, but not only under the Management Service Agreement, to Vectron Systems Fab 36 technical assistance as required in Article III (including the disclosure stated therein). Irrespective of any fault, Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding warrants by means of an independent promise of warranty pursuant to Section 311 para. 1 German Civil Code that Wafers which meet the requirements can be manufactured in a timely manner with the provided technical assistance and the rights on intellectual property granted according to Section 3 b) of the License Agreement and the technical documentation. The exclusive legal consequence of the warranty irrespective of fault is that Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding cannot invoke bad performance/non-performance by Vectron Systems Fab 36; such warranty has no other legal consequences. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Know-how to be provided pursuant to this Section 1.01(c) and Article III shall include such of the “Specific Results” (as such term is defined in the JDA), as are necessary or, in the reasonable opinion of Vectron Systems Inc., desirable, to enable Vectron Systems Fab 36 to manufacture Products on a timely basis, in accordance with the Specifications for the relevant Product. Any disclosure of Know-how and Specifications pursuant to this Section 1.01(c) or Article III (including, without limitation, Know-how consisting of Specific Results and Know-how disclosed by way of the furnishing of technical assistance) to Vectron Systems Fab 36 by or on behalf of Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding shall be subject to the License Agreement. I-2 Section 1.02. Compliance with Laws and Required Permits. (a) Vectron Systems Fab 36 shall, at its cost and expense, use commercially reasonable efforts to (i) comply with all applicable Laws relating to the manufacture and the sale of the Products, and (ii) obtain all requisite consents, authorizations, permits and approvals for the manufacturing and sale to Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding and the sale by Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding to Vectron Systems Inc. of the Products from each governmental authority having jurisdiction over the manufacture and sale of the Products by Vectron Systems Fab 36 and Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding; provided, however, that Vectron Systems Fab 36 shall not be responsible for obtaining any consents, authorizations, permits or approvals in connection with the import of Products into any territory outside Germany unless expressly otherwise agreed by Vectron Systems Fab 36; (iii) Irrespective of any fault, Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding warrants by means of an independent promise of warranty pursuant to Section 311 para. 1 German Civil Code that neither the manufacture nor the distribution of the Products is conflicting with German law. The exclusive legal consequence of the warranty irrespective of fault is that Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding cannot invoke bad performance/non-performance by Vectron Systems Fab 36; such warranty has no other legal consequences. Section 1.03. Quality of Products; Inspection and Rejection; Replacement Products . Without prejudice to Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding’s obligation to make payment for Products Shipped pursuant to Section 2.06 and Section 2.07: (a) Vectron Systems Fab 36 shall use all reasonable efforts to ensure that each Product Shipped meets the Specifications for that Product. In order to assist Vectron Systems Fab 36 in such efforts and for the purpose of assuring to Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding the quality of the Products required under this Agreement, Vectron Systems Fab 36 shall permit the duly authorized representatives of Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding and/or of Vectron Systems Inc. and its Subsidiaries, at any time during normal working hours and on reasonable notice, to inspect any premises of Vectron Systems Fab 36, and shall use all reasonable efforts to permit such representatives, at such time and on such notice, to inspect any premises of any third party where any of the Products, or any labeling or packaging for them, are manufactured by or for Vectron Systems Fab 36. (b) Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding shall promptly notify Vectron Systems Fab 36 in writing should the quality of the Products Shipped vary materially from the typical quality of the four previous shipments or, in the case of the first four shipments, from the quality of the previous shipments. In such event, Vectron Systems Fab 36 shall use its reasonable commercial efforts to restore the quality of the Products delivered hereunder to again meet such typical quality as soon as possible. I-3 ARTICLE II Purchase Price and Delivery of Products Section 2.01. Descriptive Overview of Product Pricing . Without derogating from Sections 2.02 through 2.07 hereunder: From and after the Effective Date Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding shall purchase all Products which it orders from Vectron Systems Fab 36 and are Shipped by Vectron Systems Fab 36 in accordance with the requirements of this Agreement. The purchase price for the Products will be set at a price per Wafer which will be determined on a “cost-plus” basis for any month in accordance with the Section 2 hereinafter (“ Selling Price Per Wafer ”). For all Products Shipped during any Month, Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding shall make payments to Vectron Systems Fab 36 based on the Selling Price Per Wafer. Within 25 days after the end of each Month, other than one in which no Products are Shipped, Vectron Systems Fab 36 shall determine the Selling Price Per Wafer for such Month on the basis of its Total Costs actually incurred during that Month and the Applicable Percentage in accordance with Section 2.02. The parties acknowledge that this pricing mechanism may have the result of dictating a high price per Wafer for any Month in which only relatively few Wafers are Shipped and that after the Effective Date it will result in a full cost reimbursement during each year. Section 2.02. Definition of Selling Price Per Wafer. (a) Promptly, but in any event not later than 25 days after the end of each Month, the Selling Price Per Wafer for that Month shall be calculated in accordance with the formula contained in Section 2.02(b). Such calculation shall be based on the Total Costs actually incurred during such Month and the Actual Volume for such Month. (b) For any Month the Selling Price Per Wafer shall be calculated in accordance with the following formula:- where: “ Applicable Percentage ” shall be 110%; “ AV ” means the Actual Volume for that Month; “ TC ” means Vectron Systems Fab 36’s Total Costs (as defined in Annex 1) for or in respect of that Month. I-4 Section 2.03. Advance Payment Adjustment to Reimburse Total Costs in Certain Months during which no Wafers are Shipped . For any Month after the Effective Date during which no Products are Shipped, promptly after the end but in any event not later than 30 days after such Month, Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding shall make a creditable but not repayable advance payment in the amount of Vectron Systems Fab 36’s Total Costs multiplied by the Applicable Percentage (the “ Advance Payment Adjustment Amount ”) regardless of further Wafer Shipments. If Wafers are again Shipped in any Month thereafter, the Total Costs of Vectron Systems Fab 36 shall be increased for that Month by the Total Costs of all preceding Months during which no Wafers were Shipped; the Advance Payment Adjustment Amounts shall be set-off against the payment to be made for such Month (confer definition of Total Costs in Annex 1, lit (g)). Section 2.04. Annual Compensation for Certain Administrative Costs . Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding shall compensate Vectron Systems Fab 36 for any administrative costs, including without limitation bookkeeping, legal and accounting costs, incurred by Vectron Systems Fab 36 from time to time for the direct benefit of Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding, as follows. The parties agree that the amount payable by Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding to Vectron Systems Fab 36 under this Section 2.04 shall be Euro 25,000 per annum for each Fiscal Year (which amount shall be pro-rated for any portion of a Fiscal Year) from the date hereof. Such payment shall be made not later than 30 days after the end of each Fiscal Year. Section 2.05. Shipping Terms . Vectron Systems Fab 36 shall deliver the Products purchased hereunder from Dresden, Germany. Vectron Systems Fab 36 will arrange all freight, trucking and insurance. The costs for transportation and insurance will – as far as applicable - be separately regulated by the parties on a case-by-case basis. Section 2.06. Payments; Set Off . (a) Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding shall pay to Vectron Systems Fab 36 the Selling Price Per Wafer for all Products Shipped by Vectron Systems Fab 36 within 30 days after receipt of invoices denominated in Euro from Vectron Systems Fab 36 in accordance with this Section 2.06. Vectron Systems Fab 36 shall be entitled to issue invoices to Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding on a monthly basis. Payment for all other amounts due hereunder from either party (including any adjustment amount) shall be unconditionally due on or before the date specified in this Agreement for such amount. (b) All amounts under this Agreement not paid when due from Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding shall bear interest at the rate of 3% above the three-month EURIBOR per annum on a pro rata temporis basis. Such interest shall be due and payable at the end of each calendar month. (c) Any amounts that are due from Vectron Systems Fab 36 to Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding hereunder shall be payable only in the form of credit against amounts owed or to be owed by Vectron Systems Fab 36 I-5 Holding to Vectron Systems Fab 36 under this Agreement, except to the extent provided in Section 3.04 in connection with the Fab 36 Management Service Agreement. Any amount that is due from Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding to Vectron Systems Fab 36 hereunder may be set-off against any payment then due under Sections 3, 5.02, or the Fab 36 Management Service Agreement, from Vectron Systems Fab 36 to Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding. (d) To secure the payment claims of Vectron Systems Fab 36, Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding, here and now, assigns to Vectron Systems Fab 36 its payment claims against Vectron Systems Inc. under the Fab 36 Holding Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement attached hereto as Annex A (including damage claims in case that, in the event of bankruptcy of Vectron Systems Inc., the trustee in bankruptcy of Vectron Systems Inc. or any other person with similar authority does not continue the Fab 36 Holding Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement, regardless of whether such damage claims result under German or U.S. state or federal law). Section 2.06 a). Assignment of Damage Claims. (a) Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding undertakes to purchase damage claims asserted by Vectron Systems Fab 36, which reduced the Total Costs pursuant to Section (6) (b) of Annex 1, if and to the extent that neither the damaging party nor an insurer makes any payment on account of such claims by the end of the second month after the month in which the Total Costs have been reduced. This purchase obligation does not apply in case of damage claims of less than 100,000.00 EUR. (b) The purchase price of the damage claim shall correspond to the claim asserted against the third party and shall be included by Vectron Systems Fab 36 in the next invoice after arising of the purchase obligation. (c) The assignment shall take effect at the time of sale; however, Vectron Systems Fab 36 is hereby granted authority of collection subject to any other regulation in the individual case. In the event that the assignment of the damage claim is excluded, Vectron Systems Fab 36 shall assert the claim in its own name and for the account of Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding. It shall comply with the instructions given by Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding in this connection. Section 2.07. Method of Payment . Payments under this Agreement from Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding to Vectron Systems Fab 36, if made other than by set-off permitted by Section 2.06, shall be deposited into an account of Vectron Systems Fab 36 at Dresdner Bank AG, Dresden Branch or such other account of Vectron Systems Fab 36 specified by Vectron Systems Fab 36 with the prior written consent of the Facility Agent (a copy of which consent shall be delivered by Vectron Systems Fab 36 to Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding) with not less than 15 days written notice to Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding, and all such payments shall be made in Euro, or in US-Dollar, or a combination of Euro and US-Dollar, in each case at the option of Vectron Systems Fab 36. The relevant US-Dollar amount results from the conversion of Euro to US-Dollar at the Facility Agent’s spot rate of exchange as in effect on the date of the relevant invoice. Section 2.08. Adjustments for Changes in Laws or German GAAP . In the event that there is a change in applicable Law or in German GAAP, and such change results in (i) payments I-6 due from Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding to Vectron Systems Fab 36, or due from Vectron Systems Fab 36 to Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding (as the case may be) being materially different in aggregate amount or (ii) the timing of the receipt of such payments being materially different from the payments contemplated by this Article II, the affected party shall promptly notify the other party, Vectron Systems Inc. and the Facility Agent, and Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding and Vectron Systems Fab 36 shall negotiate in good faith with a view to agreeing to such reasonable amendments to this Article II and the definitions used therein (which amendments shall be made only with the prior written consent of the Facility Agent) as are necessary as a result of such change. In order to restore the position contemplated by this Article II in the event Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding and Vectron Systems Fab 36 fail to reach agreement on such amendments within 30 days of becoming aware of such change, or if the Facility Agent reasonably withholds its consent to any proposed amendments, all payments under this Article II shall be calculated without giving effect to such change. For avoidance of doubt, any amendments of the applicable law or of the German GAAP must not lead to a reduction of the cost basis of Total Costs. Section 2.09. Product Warranty . Subject to Section 2.10, Vectron Systems Fab 36 represents and warrants that all Products sold to Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding pursuant to this Agreement shall conform in all material respects to the Specifications for such Products and, if expressly agreed by Vectron Systems Fab 36 in writing in advance, to any specific changes thereto. Section 2.10. Disclaimer . THE WARRANTY CONTAINED IN SECTION 2.09 IS THE SOLE AND EXCLUSIVE WARRANTY AS TO THE PRODUCTS AND IS EXPRESSLY IN LIEU OF ANY EXPRESS OR IMPLIED WARRANTIES, IN FACT OR IN LAW, INCLUDING, WITHOUT LIMITATION, WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY OR FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE OR ANY SIMILAR WARRANTIES IMPLIED BY APPLICABLE LAW. REPAIR OR REPLACEMENT OF DEFECTIVE PRODUCTS SHALL BE Vectron Systems FAB 36 HOLDING’S EXCLUSIVE REMEDY WITH RESPECT TO DEFECTIVE PRODUCTS. Vectron Systems FAB 36 ASSUMES NO LIABILITY IN TORT OR STRICT LIABILITY, NOR SHALL Vectron Systems FAB 36 BE LIABLE TO Vectron Systems FAB 36 HOLDING OR ANY AFFILIATE THEREOF FOR LOSS OR USE OF PRODUCTS OR ANY OTHER INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES INCURRED BY Vectron Systems FAB 36 HOLDING OR ANY SUBSIDIARY OR AFFILIATE THEREOF. IN NO EVENT SHALL THE LIABILITY OF Vectron Systems FAB 36 ARISING IN CONNECTION WITH ANY PRODUCTS SOLD HERUNDER EXCEED THE PURCHASE PRICE FOR SUCH PRODUCTS. ARTICLE III Technical Assistance Section 3.01. Provision of Know-how and Advice by or on Behalf of Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding to Vectron Systems Fab 36. (a) Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding shall from time to time disclose, or shall arrange for Vectron Systems Inc. or one or more Subsidiaries of Vectron Systems Inc. to disclose, to Vectron Systems Fab 36 such of the Know-how and Specifications of Vectron Systems Inc. and its Subsidiaries (or which may be used by Vectron Systems Inc. or its Subsidiaries), and any Improvements to such Know-how and Specifications made by Vectron Systems I-7 Inc. or one or more of its Subsidiaries after such disclosure, as is necessary or, in the judgment of Vectron Systems Inc. or the Subsidiary of Vectron Systems Inc. making such disclosure, reasonably desirable to enable Vectron Systems Fab 36 to manufacture Products on an efficient and timely basis in accordance with the Specifications for the relevant Product (such Know-how, Specifications and Improvements together being the “Intellectual Property ”). Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding shall from time to time provide, or shall arrange for Vectron Systems Inc. to provide, one (1) copy (or if requested by Vectron Systems Fab 36, two (2) copies), on a non-exclusive basis, of any Technical Documentation for such Intellectual Property in the possession of Vectron Systems Inc. or one or more of its Subsidiaries. Nothing in this Article III shall require Vectron Systems Inc. or any Subsidiary of Vectron Systems Inc. disclosing Intellectual Property to create Technical Documentation unless required for the manufacture of Wafer hereunder . (b) Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding agrees to advise, or to arrange for Vectron Systems Inc. or one or more Subsidiaries of Vectron Systems Inc., to advise Vectron Systems Fab 36, upon Vectron Systems Fab 36’s request in writing, in relation to (i) the configuration of the equipment installed or to be installed at the Plant and used for the production of each Product, and (ii) the requirements for such configuration, the installation of the equipment and the machinery in the Plant necessary for the manufacture of such Products and the calibration and testing of such equipment and machinery. (c) Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding shall from time to time permit, or shall arrange for Vectron Systems Inc. to permit, Vectron Systems Fab 36 to make such number of copies of the Technical Documentation, or any part thereof, provided or disclosed to it hereunder as Vectron Systems Fab 36 may require for the purposes of this Agreement. (d) Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding shall from time to time notify, or shall arrange for Vectron Systems Inc. to notify, Vectron Systems Fab 36 promptly of any material errors in the Intellectual Property or Technical Documentation provided or disclosed to Vectron Systems Fab 36 hereunder or in connection herewith upon Vectron Systems Inc. or one or more of its other Subsidiaries becoming aware thereof and, to the extent Vectron Systems Inc. or one or more of its Subsidiaries develops or obtains a correction for such error, shall promptly provide, or shall arrange for Vectron Systems Inc. to promptly provide, to Vectron Systems Fab 36 the Intellectual Property and/or Technical Documentation to enable Vectron Systems Fab 36 to correct such error. (e) Without derogating from Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding’s obligation to provide, or to arrange for Vectron Systems Inc. to provide, disclosure to Vectron Systems Fab 36 of such of the Know-how and Specifications of Vectron Systems Inc. and its Subsidiaries, or which Vectron Systems Inc. or its Subsidiaries have the right to use, as is necessary to enable Vectron Systems Fab 36 to manufacture Products hereunder, but notwithstanding any other provision of this Section 3.01, Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding shall not be required to disclose or arrange for Vectron Systems Inc. to disclose any Intellectual Property or Technical Documentation which Vectron Systems Inc. and its Subsidiaries – despite their best efforts - do not have a right to disclose or the disclosure of which would require the payment of compensation to a third party. Own acquisition by Vectron Systems Fab 36 is not precluded thereby. I-8 Section 3.02. Expert Support, Training and Technical Assistance. (a) Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding shall, upon Vectron Systems Fab 36’s request in writing, provide, or arrange for Vectron Systems Inc. or one or more Subsidiaries of Vectron Systems Inc. to provide, to Vectron Systems Fab 36, at the Plant, technical discussions, lectures, guidance and/or technical training in order to enable Vectron Systems Fab 36 to use, for the purposes of this Agreement, the Intellectual Property and Technical Documentation disclosed or to be disclosed to Vectron Systems Fab 36 hereunder or in connection herewith and under or in connection with the License Agreement. Such discussions, lectures, guidance and training shall be conducted by Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding and/or Vectron Systems Inc. personnel who are qualified to provide advice with respect to the relevant Intellectual Property and Technical Documentation (“ Qualified Personnel ”). (b) The timing of visits of such Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding and/or Vectron Systems Inc. personnel shall be arranged by agreement between Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding and Vectron Systems Fab 36. (c) Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding shall, upon Vectron Systems Fab 36’s request in writing, provide, or arrange for Vectron Systems Inc. to provide, to Vectron Systems Fab 36, at the Plant, all technical assistance necessary to remedy a Production Problem. Section 3.03. Access to Comparable Plants . Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding shall arrange for Vectron Systems Inc. to permit or to cause Vectron Systems Saxony to permit Vectron Systems Fab 36 to send, at the expense of Vectron Systems Fab 36 or as otherwise agreed by Vectron Systems Inc., employees of Vectron Systems Fab 36 to visit Vectron Systems Saxony’s Wafer Fabrication Plant in Dresden, Germany known as (“ Fab 30 ”) for so long as such plant is owned by Vectron Systems Saxony or another Subsidiary of Vectron Systems Inc. and Vectron Systems Inc. holds a direct or indirect controlling interest in Vectron Systems Saxony, at reasonable times for the purpose of witnessing and training in the use of any of the Intellectual Property and Technical Documentation provided or disclosed to it hereunder. Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding shall upon Vectron Systems Fab 36’s request in writing, provide, or arrange for Vectron Systems Inc. to provide, to such Vectron Systems Fab 36 employees at such Wafer Fabrication Plants technical discussions, lectures, guidance and/or technical training by Qualified Personnel in order to enable such Vectron Systems Fab 36 employees to understand the usage being made at such Wafer Fabrication Plants of the relevant Intellectual Property and Technical Documentation. Section 3.04. Charge for Technical Assistance. (a) The remuneration for services provided under this Section 3 shall be paid by Vectron Systems Fab 36 directly to Vectron Systems Inc. (real contract in favor of a third party) in accordance with the Fab 36 Management Service Agreement. (b) The foregoing notwithstanding, the parties agree that, without prejudice to the last sentence of Section 1.01(c) or to the License Agreement, any Intellectual Property provided in any form to Vectron Systems Fab 36 for its use hereunder is provided at no charge. I-9 ARTICLE IV Accounting Reports: Other Reports: Right of Inspection By or On Behalf of Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding Annual Accounting Reports from Vectron Systems Fab 36 . Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding and its duly authorized representatives (which may include duly authorized representatives of Vectron Systems Inc. or a Subsidiary of Vectron Systems Inc.) and, if the Loan Agreement Termination Date has not yet taken place, the Facility Agent shall at all reasonable times have access to the books and accounts kept by Vectron Systems Fab 36 and annually upon the closing of the Fiscal Year all such books and accounts shall be audited by a firm of independent and internationally known public accountants selected by Vectron Systems Fab 36 with the approval of Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding and, if the Loan Agreement Termination Date has not yet taken place, the Facility Agent (the consent of the Facility Agent not to be unreasonably withheld). A copy of each such report of audit shall be sent to Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding and, prior to the Loan Agreement Termination Date, the Facility Agent. Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding and its duly authorized representatives (which may include duly authorized representatives of Vectron Systems Inc. or of a Subsidiary of Vectron Systems Inc.) shall also have the right to examine and inspect at any reasonable time all properties and operations of Vectron Systems Fab 36 to which this Agreement relates. The rights of Leipziger Messe GmbH and of M+W Zander Fünfte Verwaltungsgesellschaft mbH (“ M+W ”) as direct or indirect partners of Vectron Systems Fab 36 pursuant to Article 9.3 of the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Limited Partnership Agreement remain unaffected thereby. ARTICLE V Effectiveness; Termination Section 5.01. Effectiveness: Termination. (a) This Agreement shall become effective on the date hereof and (unless otherwise extended as hereinafter provided) shall terminate on the date which is the earlier of (i) the effective date of the ordinary notice of termination given by any of the Parties with three months’ notice, such effective date being the end of any calendar month, however, not before the Loan Agreement Termination Date and (ii) the Termination Date as defined in subsection (b) below. No notice of ordinary termination may be given by Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding as long as Leipziger Messe GmbH and/or M+W are direct or indirect partners of Vectron Systems Fab 36. (b) Each of the events described in this Section 5.01(b) shall constitute good cause for terminating this Agreement. If a good cause for terminating this Agreement pursuant to clauses (iv) through (xi) hereinafter has occurred and is continuing, Vectron Systems Fab 36 may give notice of termination for good cause. Prior to the Loan Agreement Termination Date, the Security Agent pursuant to and in accordance with the Security Documents may, in the event of a good cause pursuant to any of the clauses (viii), (x) or (xi), give notice of termination in all cases in accordance with the power of attorney attached as Annex 2. In the event of a good cause pursuant to any of the clauses (iv), (v) or (vii), Vectron Systems Inc. shall be entitled to give notice of such termination in all cases in accordance with the power of attorney attached hereto as Annex 3. I-10 The right of Vectron Systems Fab 36 to revoke Vectron Systems Inc.’s power of attorney for good cause shall remain unaffected In addition, this Agreement shall automatically terminate (without any requirement for any action by any party hereto) immediately upon the occurrence of the good cause for terminating this Agreement described in clauses (i) through (iii) below. Upon termination for good cause. this Agreement shall terminate on the date any such notice is given; upon automatic termination, this Agreement shall terminate upon the occurrence of the event (the respective date being the “Termination Date ”). Good causes for terminating this Agreement are as follows: (i) the expropriation or condemnation of the Plant or any substantial part of the assets or business of Vectron Systems Fab 36 or Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding by any Governmental Authority, or the involuntary suspension, or curtailment below seventy-five percent of capacity, by Vectron Systems Fab 36 of the operation of the Plant for six months or more as a result of any change in or introduction of any Law occurring after the date hereof; (ii) Vectron Systems Inc. or Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding being required by any Governmental Authority to divest itself of all or a substantial portion of its direct or indirect interest in Vectron Systems Fab 36, or Vectron Systems Fab 36 being required by any Governmental Authority to divest itself of all or a substantial portion of the Plant; (iii) the destruction of the Plant or a substantial portion thereof and a decision by Vectron Systems Fab 36 not to rebuild the same after having received insurance proceeds in respect of such destruction in an amount at least equal to the depreciated book value of such Plant or substantial portion thereof; (iv) acceleration of the liabilities of Vectron Systems Fab 36 after expiration of an applicable additional period as a result of occurrence of an event as specified in the Section of the Loan Agreement relating to the termination of the loan entitling the Banks to terminate the Loan Agreement and/or if the Facility Agent, the Security Agent or any of the Banks asserts a right under any of the Security Documents (as defined in the Loan Agreement); (v) the involuntary (including without limitation as a result of enforcement of any rights of security granted in shares of Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding and/or Vectron Systems Fab 36 or performance of any undertakings to transfer such shares made to secure obligations of Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding and/or Vectron Systems Fab 36) transfer of a controlling interest in Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding and/or Vectron Systems Fab 36 from Vectron Systems Inc. and its Subsidiaries to one or more third parties is threatened and bankruptcy proceedings are not opened over the assets of Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding and/or Vectron Systems Inc. within one (1) month from transfer of such controlling interest; (vi) [deliberately blank]; (vii) any event which (A) results in the termination of Vectron Systems Inc.’s rights to disclosure to Vectron Systems Fab 36 as contemplated by Section 1.01 and Article III, or (B) otherwise terminates or makes impracticable the manufacture of Wafers as contemplated hereunder and bankruptcy proceedings are not opened over the assets of Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding and/or Vectron Systems Inc. within one (1) month from occurrence of the event; I-11 (viii) failure of Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding or Vectron Systems Inc. to make any payment required from it hereunder this Agreement or under the Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding Cost Plus Reimbursement Agreement within forty-five (45) days of the date due therefor or default by Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding or Vectron Systems Inc. in the performance of or compliance with any other term contained in this Agreement or any such other agreement and such default shall not have been remedied within thirty (30) days after receipt of notice of such default from Vectron Systems Fab 36, or if the Loan Agreement Termination Date has not yet taken place, the Facility Agent and bankruptcy proceedings are not opened over the assets of Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding and/or Vectron Systems Inc. within one (1) month from fulfillment of the aforesaid conditions. (ix) a [deliberately blank]; (x) execution is issued ( Einleitung der Zwangsvollstreckung ) against all or a substantial part of the assets of Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding unless such execution is withdrawn within three weeks of the issuance thereof; realization of such execution; or the realization of execution on the equity shares in Vectron Systems Fab 36 owned by Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding and bankruptcy proceedings are not opened over the assets of Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding and/or Vectron Systems Inc. within one (1) month from fulfillment of the aforesaid conditions; . (xi) the discontinuance of payments generally ( Zahlungseinstellung ) by Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding and bankruptcy proceedings are not opened over the assets of Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding and/or Vectron Systems Inc. within one (1) month from discontinuance of payments; Notice of termination may be given within forty-five (45) days from occurrence of the respective termination event. The right to assert statutory damage claims (Section 314 (4) BGB [German Civil Code] in conjunction with Sections 280 et seq. BGB) on the ground of termination of this Agreement for the reasons set out in sub-sect. b) above remains unaffected. Section 5.02. Rights Upon Termination. (a) Promptly, but in no event later than 20 days following the Termination Date, (i) the actual Selling Price for the Month ending on the Termination Date shall be calculated in accordance with Section 2.02; and (ii) if no Products were Shipped in the Month ending on the Termination Date, the final Advance Payment Adjustment Amount for that Month shall be calculated in accordance with Section 2.03. (b) Upon termination of this Agreement, Vectron Systems Fab 36 Holding shall purchase all inventory and work-in-process of Vectron Systems Fab 36 as of the Termination Date. The purchase price shall be equal to the value thereof as shown in the books of Vectron Systems Fab 36 in accordance with German GAAP. I-12 (c) Upon termination of this Agreement, all amounts shall be offset between the parties and the balance shall be settled by payment. Section 5.03. Survival . The provisions of Sections 2.09 and 2.10, 5.2, 6.02, 6.03 and 6.06 shall survive any termination of this Agreement. ARTICLE VI Miscellaneous Section 6.01. Force Majeure . (a) A party to this Agreement shall not be liable for the consequences of any failure to perform, or default in performing, any of its obligations, other than its payment obligations, under this Agreement if that party can show that such failure is caused by Force Majeure. In the event that no Wafers can be manufactured due to Force Majeure, the payment obligations hereunder shall continue to be owed. (b) In case of subsection (a), the said failure shall not be considered non-compliance with any term or condition of this Agreement, and all the obligations (other than payment obligations) and times which because of such Force Majeure could not be fulfilled shall be deemed to have been suspended while the Force Majeure continues. In addition, the party for whom such obligations and/or times have been suspended shall be entitled to take reasonable steps during the pendency of the relevant Force Majeure to limit its losses resulting from such Force Majeure, and following the occurrence of such Force Majeure such obligations and/or times shall continue to be suspended for such further reasonable period as is necessary for such party to restore its capacity to perform such obligations and/or meet such times. Section 6.02. Applicable Law . This Agreement is governed by the law of the Federal Republic of Germany. Application of the UN Convention on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods of 11 April 1980 is expressly excluded. Section 6.03. Dispute Resolution . a) The parties shall endeavor to reach an amicable settlement of any dispute arising out of or in connection with this Agreement and its implementation, including any question regarding its existence, validity, termination or its performance, or in connection with arrangements regarding the performance of this Agreement. b) If any party declares that a reasonable attempt at amicable settlement of a dispute mentioned in sub-section a) has failed, such dispute shall be finally resolved by arbitration, to the exclusion of the courts of regular jurisdiction, under the Rules of Arbitration ( Schiedsgerichtsordnung ) of the German Arbitration Board (“Deutsche Institution für Schiedsgerichtswesen e.V.”), by three arbitrators. The place of arbitration shall be Dresden, Germany. The German Code of Civil Procedure shall apply where the Rules of Arbitration are silent. I-13 c) The language to be used in the arbitration proceedings shall be German and all documents written in a language other than German shall be translated into the German language for the arbitration proceedings, unless otherwise decided by the parties to the arbitration proceedings. d) The arbitral awards shall be substantiated in writing. The arbitration tribunal shall decide on the matter of costs of the arbitration and on the allocation of expenditure among the respective parties to the arbitration proceedings. Section 6.04. Definitions A list with definitions of the terms used in this Agreement is attached hereto. It is the will of the parties hereto that each of the terms listed in the Annex 1 has the meaning specified there unless the context requires a different interpretation. Section 6.05. Assignment None of the parties hereto shall have the right to assign its rights hereunder without the prior written consent of the other party hereto, of Vectron Systems Inc., of Leipziger Messe GmbH and of M+W as long as they are limited partners of Vectron Systems Fab 36 and, prior to the Loan Agreement Termination Date, of the Security Agent. However, Vectron Systems Fab 36 may assign its rights under this Agreement to the Security Agent and/or the Banks as security for obligations of Vectron Systems Fab 36 under the Loan Agreement, and the Security Agent may in turn assign such rights to any direct transferee of the Plant in the context of the proper realization of such security. Section 6.06. Language The German version of this Agreement shall be controlling. Section 6.07 Final Provisions a) No modifications or amendments to this Agreement shall be binding unless in writing. This requirement of written form may only be waived in writing. b) If present or future terms of this Agreement are invalid or unenforceable in whole or in part or lose their validity or enforceability at a later date, this shall not affect the validity of the remaining provisions hereof. The same shall apply insofar as it transpires that there is a gap in this agreement. The invalid or unenforceable provision shall be replaced, or the gap filled, by a reasonable provision which, to the extent legally permissible, comes closest to what the contracting parties intended or would have intended in accordance with the meaning and purpose of this Agreement, had they considered the point upon I-14 conclusion hereof. This also applies if the invalidity of a provision results from a degree of performance standardized hereunder (set period, date); in such cases a legally permissible degree of performance or time (set period, date), coming as close as possible to the intentions, shall replace what was agreed. In case of disputes arising among the parties hereto due to subsections a) and b) above, the arbitration tribunal (Section 6.03) shall be entitled to determine with binding force the provision which is to apply thereunder.
